title: Lithocarpus gelinicus
intro: Lithocarpus gelinicus, also known as the Gelincara tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. The health benefits of Lithocarpus gelinicus include its potential to exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can contribute to the treatment of various diseases such as cancer, diabetes, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lithocarpus gelinicus is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, as well as being extracted into oils and tinctures, to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Lithocarpus gelinicus is classified within the family Fagaceae. This family comprises beech and oak trees. As a member of the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus gelinicus is a type of evergreen tree. Its classification also places it within the order Fagales, which includes several species of trees known for their deciduous or evergreen leaves. This classification provides insight into the evolutionary relationships of Lithocarpus gelinicus with other trees in the Fagaceae family.
benefits: Lithocarpus gelinicus is a lesser-known species with potentially significant health benefits, although more research is required to confirm its effects. properties: Lithocarpus gelinicus is a plant species that has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, and it is reported to have therapeutic properties. constituents: Lithocarpus gelinicus contains several active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins. The plant also contains various terpenoids and triterpenoids, which have been reported to have potential medicinal uses.
